# Residual false positives the guard cannot resolve.
#
# Every string here is a legitimate non-secret that the entropy heuristic
# will still flag because it is structurally indistinguishable from a
# real base64-encoded secret. The allow-list (II4) only suppresses
# matches that fit a NAMED benign shape (Git SHA, UUID, semver, SRI
# hash). Base64 of short ASCII plaintext has no syntactic tell.
#
# Decoded content is given for reviewer context only. The guard never
# decodes candidate strings — doing so at ingress would open a
# zip-bomb / resource-exhaustion vector and still not resolve the core
# ambiguity (is "dGVzdA==" → "test" a leak of a 4-char password?).
#
# Policy: these reject at ingress. Operators who need them in memory
# either (a) wrap them in a context that isn't quoted, (b) set
# MEMORY_SECRET_POLICY=redact locally, or (c) split them across keys
# below the min-length threshold.

# base64("agent-memory-is-not-a-secret-123456")
YWdlbnQtbWVtb3J5LWlzLW5vdC1hLXNlY3JldC0xMjM0NTY=

# base64("this-is-just-a-test-string-for-corpus")
dGhpcy1pcy1qdXN0LWEtdGVzdC1zdHJpbmctZm9yLWNvcnB1cw==
